issue.body:
Add `postclassify_decoration(layout, page) -> PageLayout` in `pd_book_tools/layout/geometry.py`, run as a late pass in `Page.reorganize_page` after `tag_words_with_layout`. Heuristic: a `figure` region that is small (<5% of page area), positioned near top or bottom, and contains no detected text words should be reclassified as `decoration`.

PP-DocLayout has no `decoration` class; today the adapter maps `seal -> RegionType.decoration` and falls back to `image -> figure` for ornamental woodcuts (chapter headpieces, fleurons). This heuristic helps the illustration extractor downstream pick the right `type=decoration` for `i_<stem>_NN.png` filenames.

Out of scope until the layout fine-tune lands; once the model has a first-class `decoration` head this becomes a fallback rather than the primary classifier.

Source: docs/ROADMAP.md (Open - layout consumption).
Spec: docs/specs/03-reorganize-pipeline.md

## Acceptance
- [ ] `postclassify_decoration` implemented in `layout/geometry.py` with the area/position/no-text heuristic
- [ ] Wired into `Page.reorganize_page` after `tag_words_with_layout`
- [ ] At least one fixture exercises a small top-of-page no-text figure being reclassified to `decoration`
- [ ] Existing reorganize fixtures unchanged (or diffs reviewed and accepted)

issue.title: Decoration-vs-figure post-classification heuristic

issue.body:
## Summary

The ship-issue bot, while shipping issues #8/#9/#10/#12 to wip/ship-issue, appended an `### Issue #22 — 2026-05-10 17:27 UTC / Closes #22` section to PR #17's body. Issue #22 ("Child A: test triage-fork.py pointer comment on parent") was already closed and is unrelated to coverage work. Had this PR been merged as-is, GitHub would have re-processed the `Closes #22` (no-op on already-closed issue, but signals the bot wrote bad metadata).

## Repro

`gh pr view 17 -R ConcaveTrillion/pd-book-tools --json body --jq .body` (before R4 cleanup at 2026-05-11) showed the orphan section.

## Root cause hypothesis

The bot's success.sh appends a PR-body section based on whatever issue number was passed via `ISSUE_JSON` or env var. If that number was leftover from a prior aborted cycle, or read from a stale .ship-issue-tmp/ file, the bot appends without validating.

## Suggested fix

In `scripts/ship-issue-success.sh` (or the skill's PR-body-update step):
1. Validate the issue number belongs to the current repo: `gh issue view $N -R $REPO --json state,number` — must succeed and state must be OPEN.
2. If state is CLOSED or the gh call fails, refuse to append the section + log to stderr; the orchestrator should treat this as a bounce.

## Acceptance

- [ ] Bot refuses to append a PR-body section for issues that are already CLOSED at success time.
- [ ] Bot refuses to append a PR-body section for issues from a different repo.
- [ ] Regression test covers both failure modes.

## Surfaced by

PR #17 static review (R4 closeout, finding J-4).
issue.title: ship-issue: bot appended unrelated/closed issue #22 to PR #17 body

## Context

Parent feature-request: #2 (https://github.com/ConcaveTrillion/pd-book-tools/issues/2)

This spec issue tracks the design + decomposition of the parent feature-request via:

1. `/spec-from-issue 277940NEW` — produces `docs/specs/NN-<slug>.md` (the design spec) + draft PR.
2. `/decompose-spec docs/specs/NN-<slug>.md` — creates per-spec milestone + child issues with `bot:ship-issue-ready` where appropriate.

## Triage decision

Approved as needing-spec during the 2026-05-11 backlog triage sweep — this feature requires design work before implementation; not a one-PR tracking-style chore.

Inherits effort/model labels from the parent feature-request.

Spec: (to be filled by /spec-from-issue)

issue.body:
Filed from the pd-book-tools deep code/security review.

Report: `docs/research/2026-05-22-deep-code-security-review.md`
Finding: #2

Moved from mistakenly filed meta issue: ConcaveTrillion/ocr-container-meta#295

Evidence:

- `pd_book_tools/ocr/page.py:3322`
- `pd_book_tools/geometry/bounding_box.py:118`
- `pd_book_tools/ocr/page.py:3433`

Detection export always calls `word.bounding_box.get_four_point_scaled_polygon_list(img_width, img_height)`, and that helper multiplies coordinates by image dimensions. Pixel-space boxes such as `(10,10)-(20,20)` on a 100px image become `(1000,1000)-(2000,2000)`. Recognition export unconditionally calls `bbox.scale(...)`, which is invalid for pixel-space boxes.

Impact:

- Training data generated from Tesseract or other pixel-space OCR is corrupt or crashes.
- Bad labels can poison downstream model training.

Remediation:

- Branch on `bbox.is_normalized`.
- Scale normalized boxes; pass pixel boxes through after clamping/casting.
- Add detection and recognition export tests using `is_normalized=False`.

---
This issue was filed from a read-only review. Confirm behavior with a focused failing test before implementation where practical.
issue.title: pd-book-tools: pixel-space pages corrupt/crash DocTR training-set export

issue.body:
**Spec:** docs/specs/2026-05-24-hyphen-ngrams-sqlite.md

Add `pd_book_tools.hyphen_ngrams.HyphenNgramsClient` Protocol with `SqliteClient`
(pre-indexed, download-on-first-use from a GitHub Release asset) and `JsonApiClient`
(unofficial Google Books fallback). Replaces the unofficial JSON endpoint with a
deterministic local SQLite for hyphen-pair frequency data.

**Gates:** pd-prep-for-pgdp Stage 15 SQLite migration (Decision #2 in design-handoff spec).
**Design source:** `pd-ui/docs/templates/design_handoff_pd_ui/wf05/NOTES.md`.
